description: Execute a compiled case through the selected runtime backend while preserving deterministic inputs, explicit source labels, and hard failures for missing UE configuration or artifacts.
physical_assumptions: UE is the production renderer and physics execution backend when configured.
Fallback or SIM_PROXY execution is only a deterministic debug adapter and must remain source-labeled.
Runtime failures must not be silently converted into fallback successes.
The runtime backend owns trajectory, contact, camera, render, and log artifact collection.
verifier_rules: UE preflight failures are configuration failures, not physics failures.
Completed runs contain trajectory, contact events, camera trajectory, and render manifest.
No backend=ue run may silently invoke fallback.
repair_suggestions: Set SIM_STUDIO_UE_PROJECT, SIM_STUDIO_UE_EXECUTABLE, SIM_STUDIO_ASSET_REGISTRY, and SIM_STUDIO_UE_RUNNER_CMD for UE runs.
Inspect ue_preflight_report.json before changing case physics.
Do not use fallback artifacts to claim production UE success.
